hyperedges.label: ETL Pipeline Flow
hyperedges.description: The core ETL pipeline: data sources feed into data federation, which feeds into transaction labeling via OpenAI, validated by data integrity checks, then written to Google Sheets

hyperedges.label: LLM Prompt Composition System
hyperedges.description: The generic prompt template is composed with either expenses or incomes category schemas to form complete classification prompts sent to OpenAI

hyperedges.label: Developer Experience Toolchain
hyperedges.description: Lefthook orchestrates git hooks that run Biome for formatting/linting, commitlint for conventional commits, and TSC for type checking, while release-please and CodeQL operate on merge

nodes.label: Finance Management Tool (ETL)
nodes.description: Intelligent ETL tool that automatically fetches, categorizes, and organizes bank transactions using AI-powered classification, integrating with Google Sheets

nodes.label: David Zoufaly
nodes.description: Project author and copyright holder, MIT License 2025

nodes.label: Fio Bank
nodes.description: Bank data source accessed via API token with read-only access, token valid up to 6 months

nodes.label: Air Bank
nodes.description: Bank data source accessed via monthly PDF statement email attachments, password-protected with phone number

nodes.label: OpenAI Integration
nodes.description: Transaction categorization using OpenAI API with configurable models for dev and production environments

nodes.label: Google Sheets Integration
nodes.description: Data destination with service account authentication, separate sheets for dev and production, requires Sheets API enabled

nodes.label: GitHub Actions Automation
nodes.description: Monthly automated ETL process triggered on 1st of month at noon, sends email notification with financial summary after run

nodes.label: Generic LLM Prompt
nodes.description: Core prompt instructing LLM to act as financial analyst, learn from existing labeled transactions, classify new ones using category hierarchy, output valid JSON with transaction arrays and token count

nodes.label: Expense Categories
nodes.description: 9 top-level expense categories (A-I) with 50+ subcategories covering travel, household, gifts, car, motorcycle, garage, personal spending, land/property, and financial obligations

nodes.label: Income Categories
nodes.description: 7 income categories (INC_1 to INC_7): salary from GoodData, bonus, compensation, personal transfers (Lucie Novotna), rent (Ivo Zoufaly), debt repayments, and dividends

nodes.label: Expense Categories Template
nodes.description: Generic template with placeholder categories A/B and subcategories for users to customize their own expense category scheme

nodes.label: Income Categories Template
nodes.description: Generic template with placeholder income categories INC_1 to INC_5 for users to customize

nodes.label: Monthly Financial Email Report
nodes.description: HTML email with transaction summary (expenses, incomes, investments), financial analysis (net income, savings rate, expense ratio), Google Sheet link, and workflow details

nodes.label: Data Federation
nodes.description: Aggregation of transaction data from multiple bank sources into unified format

nodes.label: Transaction Labeling
nodes.description: AI-powered assignment of category identifiers to transactions using OpenAI

nodes.label: Data Integrity Validation
nodes.description: Post-labeling validation checking number of transactions and transaction values match

nodes.label: Cleanup Mechanism
nodes.description: Automatic cleanup on failure; manual cleanup available via yarn cleanup:all to delete last month data and mark emails unread

nodes.label: Conventional Commits
nodes.description: Commit message convention enforced by commitlint in commit-msg hook

nodes.label: Release Please
nodes.description: Automated release management via GitHub Action, creates release PRs, bumps version, generates changelog based on conventional commits

nodes.label: Biome
nodes.description: Code formatting and quality checking tool used in pre-commit hook

nodes.label: Lefthook
nodes.description: Git hooks manager configured via lefthook.yaml

nodes.label: Typedoc
nodes.description: Documentation generator requiring all objects to be exported; hosted via GitHub Pages

nodes.label: New Years Guide
nodes.description: Annual checklist: sanity check last month, progress quarter, update overview investments Q1-Q4, fork sheet for new year, create wrapped slide, update categories in expenses/incomes, reset data, set budgeting goals, check OpenAI budget and model

nodes.label: Changelog
nodes.description: Version history from 1.0.0 to 2.1.0, tracking features like email body, Zod validation, LLM retries, integration test refactor, and sheet improvements

nodes.label: TODO / 2DO
nodes.description: Future plans including Node v25 support, ML-based classification, adding KB/Revolut/Ceska Sporitelna banks, squash-merge conventional commits enforcement

nodes.label: KB Bank (Planned)
nodes.description: Planned future data source: Komercni Banka transactions

nodes.label: Revolut (Planned)
nodes.description: Planned future data source: Revolut transactions

nodes.label: Ceska Sporitelna (Planned)
nodes.description: Planned future data source: Ceska Sporitelna transactions

nodes.label: ML Classification Idea
nodes.description: Idea to use machine learning techniques for transaction classification with LLM only for edge cases, and potentially a custom LLM trained on prior years

nodes.label: Rationale: Separate Dev/Prod Sheets
nodes.description: Recommendation to use two separate Google Sheets (dev and prod) to isolate testing from production data

nodes.label: Rationale: Export All Objects for Typedoc
nodes.description: All objects must be exported even if single-module use, otherwise Typedoc excludes them from generated documentation
nodes.norm_label: rationale: export all objects for typedoc

nodes.label: Rationale: Relative Imports in Tests
nodes.description: Vitest may execute files outside test scope, so .test.ts files should use relative imports instead of @ alias to avoid resolution issues

nodes.label: Rationale: 150 Transaction Limit for LLM
nodes.description: Number of transactions sent to LLM is limited to 150 to manage token costs and context window

nodes.label: Rationale: Gmail App Password
nodes.description: Gmail requires app password instead of basic auth because Google does not support basic authentication; alternative is Seznam.cz which supports basic auth

nodes.label: GoodData
nodes.description: Employer of David Zoufaly, source of salary (INC_1), bonus (INC_2), and compensation (INC_3) income categories

nodes.label: MIT License
nodes.description: Project licensed under MIT License, copyright 2025 David Zoufaly

nodes.label: Zod
nodes.description: Schema validation library integrated in v1.0.0 for data validation

nodes.label: CodeQL
nodes.description: Code quality analysis triggered on merge to main branch

properties.description: Google Sheets financial overview dashboard with multiple sections for expenses, income, savings, investments, and budget tracking with bar charts and percentage indicators

Tracks quarterly expenses (Q1-Q4) with plan vs actual values, YTD totals, and QTD/plan percentage (135.8%, 42.6%). Contains horizontal bar chart showing quarterly expense breakdown and vertical bar chart showing QTD vs plan comparison.
Tracks quarterly income (Q1-Q4) with plan vs actual values, YTD totals, and QTD/plan percentage (84.8%, 16.0%). Contains horizontal bar chart and vertical bar chart for income comparison.
Shows expense plan fulfillment YTD/plan percentages (135.8%, 42.6%, 61.0%) with bar charts comparing planned vs actual expenses

nodes.label: Prijmy vs Vydaje (Income vs Expenses) Chart

properties.description: Comparison bar chart showing income (blue/green) vs expenses (orange) side by side across quarters, with a legend indicating Prijmy and Vydaje categories

nodes.norm_label: prijmy vs vydaje (income vs expenses) chart

properties.description: Tracks investment data with quarterly breakdown (Q1-Q4), showing values like 75.0, 40.0, 19.5, 15.5, 20.0, 14.0, 8.0. Contains horizontal bar chart for quarterly investment amounts.
Shows Pocet kategorii (category count), budget allocation with values 15 and 9, and percentage fulfillment (107.0%). Includes columns for Top kat. (top categories) with QTD ABS, QTD REL values.
Tracks savings with values like 120.0, -29.6%, -39.0, 50.0%, 50.0%, 170.0, 8.0. Shows a trend chart with red bars indicating negative savings trend (-79.0%). Contains QTD vs YTD comparison.

properties.interpretation: Savings trend sharply negative, significant shortfall vs target

properties.description: Personal finance management system that aggregates transaction data and produces this overview dashboard

properties.rationale: This image is stored in the finance-mgn repo's static/images directory, strongly suggesting the system populates or references this dashboard
Savings (penize useteno) is typically calculated as income minus expenses, so it depends on expense data
Savings (penize useteno) is typically calculated as income minus expenses, so it depends on income data
Expenses at 135.8% of plan combined with income at only 84.8% of plan directly contributes to the -79% savings trend
Below-plan income at 84.8% combined with over-plan expenses contributes to the sharply negative savings trend
